behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is request-builder -> API-client handoff (not just one pure function). A local in-process stub API can assert outgoing payload contains all labels. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; (b) no dependence on model output quality; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM-stage handoff logic. Rejected System because behavior text is about request contents, which a stub captures exactly.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a stubbed API client, call classify with a taxonomy, and assert the emitted request payload always includes the full label list (including ordering/format rules the provider expects).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is often at response-parse -> normalization -> public return boundary. A stubbed API response with an out-of-taxonomy label validates module wiring and fallback behavior. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network needed; (b) correctness does not depend on model quality, only supplied label value; (c) no multi-step agent pipeline; (d) no planner/tool/critic chain. Rejected Agentic because behavior text checks guardrail mapping of invalid value, not whether model chooses correct labels.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API client that returns an invalid label string; invoke classify end-to-end through the module and assert the caller receives exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is a deterministic normalization property on identical inputs, within one local function/object boundary and no I/O. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly scopes to normalization of identical raw responses, which can be tested directly without module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization with the same raw response fixture repeatedly and assert identical output label each run (including casing/whitespace variants if part of normalization rules).

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live calls and consistency tied to provider responses; this depends on real model behavior/non-determinism. A mock would hide model-driven inconsistency. Rejected Workflow because calls are repeated checks of one agentic decision, not a multi-stage agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real API classifications for the same document/taxonomy, log raw provider responses and final labels, and assert label drift only occurs when provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is budget-enforcement logic interacting with dispatch path (e.g., accidental send before truncate/reject). In-process wiring with stub client can prove enforcement happens pre-dispatch. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network required; (b) no dependence on model output quality; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM-stage orchestration correctness. Rejected System because behavior text says "before dispatch," which is verifiable without real transport.
behaviors.test_description: Configure small token budget, run classify with oversized input against a stub client, and assert policy behavior (truncate or reject) occurs and dispatch observes only policy-compliant payload (or no dispatch on reject).

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Requires real provider token accounting from an AI API call; mocks can trivially pass and miss true usage behavior. Although assertion is numeric, execution path depends on real model/provider behavior and reported usage metadata. Rejected System-only classification because this is not just transport/HTTP behavior; correctness hinges on real AI service outputs/usage accounting.
behaviors.test_description: Send a representative document to the real provider with production request format, read provider-reported prompt/completion/total tokens, and assert total <= configured budget under expected policy.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry/backoff orchestration across internal modules (error handling, delay policy, retry loop). A stub client that returns rate-limit then success can reproduce this without real network. Integration-brake: (a) real OS/network not required; (b) no dependence on model output quality; (c) no multi-agentic sequence; (d) no LLM handoff correctness between agentic stages. Rejected System because behavior text targets control flow on rate-limit signal, which stubs can emulate precisely.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API client scripted to return rate-limit error then success; verify module waits according to backoff/retry policy and eventually returns success instead of surfacing the initial error.
